name: Engagespot In-App Notification API
what_it_does: In-app notification infrastructure API with pre-built notification center UI component — enabling developers to add real-time in-app notifications, push, email, and SMS to applications through REST API with embeddable notification inbox widget.
use_cases: Adding in-app notification center to web apps without building custom infrastructure
Sending multi-channel notifications (in-app + push + email) through unified API
Embedding pre-built notification inbox UI component in React/Vue applications
Triggering real-time notifications from agent workflows via REST API
Managing user notification preferences and read states programmatically
not_for: Pure email marketing campaigns (use Klaviyo or Mailchimp)
Complex notification workflow automation requiring advanced branching
Enterprise-scale notifications at millions of users per month on free tier
best_when: A developer needs to add in-app notification center with minimal effort using pre-built UI components and a simple API without building notification infrastructure.
avoid_when: You need complex workflow automation, advanced segmentation, or pure email/SMS without in-app notification requirement.

auth.notes: API key and secret from Engagespot dashboard. HMAC user token generation for frontend security. Separate public/private key model — frontend uses signed user token, backend uses API key.

pricing.notes: Free tier generous for small apps. Pricing by monthly active users. Pre-built UI component saves development time. Good for SaaS startups adding notification features quickly.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per plan; documented in dashboard

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API simple and well-documented. HMAC token signing for security adds setup complexity. Frontend SDK handles real-time updates via WebSocket. Error responses include descriptive messages.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Notification sends create new notifications. Read/unread state updates are idempotent. User creation/update is upsert-style. Webhooks support event deduplication.
known_agent_gotchas: HMAC user token signing required for frontend security — backend must generate tokens
In-app component requires frontend SDK — backend-only agents cannot render notification UI
Channel credentials (FCM, APNS) required before push notifications work
Open-source self-hosted version available but less documented than cloud

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. HMAC signing prevents frontend token spoofing. GDPR compliant. API key and secret separation reduces exposure risk. Open-source core available for self-hosted data control.
